companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: NVIDIA filed its Annual Meeting Form 8-K today (June 30) with proxy voting results from the June 24 virtual meeting. NVIDIA Halos for Robotics — the industry's first full-stack AI safety system for physical AI — was announced this week. Vera Rubin runs 81% of TOP500 supercomputers; NVIDIA technology powers 90% of new TOP500 entries. FY2026 revenue: $215.9B (+65% YoY).

items.headline: NVIDIA Annual Meeting Form 8-K filed today; FY2026 revenue $215.9B (+65% YoY)
items.detail: NVIDIA filed its Form 8-K with Annual Meeting proxy voting results today (June 30), following the June 24 virtual stockholder meeting. Shareholders voted on 10 director elections, executive compensation, PwC ratification for fiscal 2027, and four shareholder proposals. FY2026 highlights from the proxy: revenue $215.9 billion (+65% YoY), gross margin 71.1%, operating income $130.4 billion, diluted EPS $4.90 (+67%). Over 90% of CEO target pay remains at-risk and 100% of CEO equity is in performance stock units.

items.headline: NVIDIA Halos for Robotics launched — industry's first full-stack AI safety system for physical AI
items.detail: NVIDIA announced NVIDIA Halos for Robotics, the industry's first full-stack, comprehensive safety system for robotics and physical AI. Halos unifies AI compute and safety architecture across the entire robot stack, providing a coherent framework from perception to actuation for safe deployment of autonomous systems at scale.

items.headline: NVIDIA runs 81% of TOP500; BioNeMo Agent Toolkit live; 35 European Vera Rubin HPC systems in development
items.detail: NVIDIA technology runs 81% of the TOP500 supercomputers, with 90% of new entries powered by NVIDIA hardware. At ISC High Performance 2026 in Hamburg, NVIDIA launched the BioNeMo Agent Toolkit enabling AI agents to gather evidence, reason across findings, run computational experiments, and accelerate scientific discovery. A record 35 AI HPC systems using the Vera Rubin platform are in development across European research institutions.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Pro officially missed its June 30 self-imposed deadline and is now delayed to July 2026 — Google DeepMind's first publicly missed model window this year. Google is refining coding, token efficiency, and long-task performance before release; no official date set. The delay directly impacts Apple's Siri AI GA timeline (September 2026). Five senior DeepMind researchers departed in one week earlier this month. Alphabet's $84.75B equity raise closes June as the largest in corporate history.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro misses June 30 deadline — officially delayed to July 2026
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro missed its self-imposed June 30 launch window, making it Google DeepMind's first publicly missed model deadline in 2026. Google is reviewing early enterprise tester feedback and refining coding, token consumption, and long-task performance before general release. The model had been in limited Vertex AI enterprise preview. No official Google statement confirmed the delay; a spokesperson declined to comment. Expected specs remain: 2M token context, Deep Think reasoning, frontier multimodal. The slip adds delivery risk to Apple's Gemini-powered Siri AI ahead of September 2026 GA.

items.headline: Gemini Managed Agents API in public preview; Antigravity Agent launched for autonomous agentic tasks
items.detail: Google launched Managed Agents in the Gemini API in public preview, enabling developers to build and deploy stateful autonomous agents running in secure, isolated Google-hosted Linux sandbox environments. The Antigravity Agent — Google's first managed agent — can autonomously plan, reason, write and execute code, manage files, and browse the web inside its sandbox. Gemini 3.5 Flash remains available for agents and coding ahead of Pro's delayed full launch.

items.headline: Five senior DeepMind researchers departed in one week; Alphabet lost ~$270B in market cap
items.detail: Google DeepMind's worst talent week of 2026: Nobel Prize winner John Jumper (AlphaFold2 co-creator, to Anthropic), Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer (reportedly to OpenAI), and researchers Jonas Adler, Alexander Pritzel, and Arthur Conmy (all to Anthropic) departed within a single week. Alphabet's stock fell roughly 7% across two trading sessions, erasing approximately $270 billion in market capitalization. The talent losses compounded the Gemini 3.5 Pro delay and delivery risk for Apple's Gemini-powered Siri.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Copilot Cowork billing activates tomorrow (July 1) — tenants that did not configure usage-based billing lose access. Microsoft 365 commercial pricing also increases July 1. MAI-Code-1, MAI-Code-1-Flash, and MAI-Thinking-1 (35B) remain the in-house model lineup from Build 2026. AWS Summit DC announced a $1B FDE program explicitly inspired by Palantir's AI deployment model.

items.headline: Copilot Cowork billing activates July 1 (tomorrow); Microsoft 365 pricing increases July 1
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork's Frontier preview grace period expires tomorrow (July 1). Tenants that have not configured usage-based billing in the M365 Admin Center will lose Cowork access until billing is set up. Microsoft 365 Business Standard and Business Premium with Copilot formalize as permanent commercial SKUs on July 1 alongside broader commercial suite price increases across Enterprise, Business, and Frontline tiers. Work IQ APIs and Microsoft IQ are GA across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio.

items.headline: MAI-Code-1, MAI-Code-1-Flash, and MAI-Thinking-1 (35B) from Build 2026; Majorana 2 quantum milestone
items.detail: Microsoft unveiled MAI-Code-1 — its inaugural code-generation model from written descriptions, tuned for GitHub and VS Code — alongside MAI-Code-1-Flash (faster, lower cost) and MAI-Thinking-1 (35B parameters, enterprise agentic reasoning). Seven new MAI models total were announced at Build 2026. Majorana 2 achieves a 20-second average qubit lifetime — a 1,000x reliability improvement. Azure Cobalt 200 VMs deliver 50% performance improvement for agentic AI workloads.

items.headline: Amazon's $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program explicitly modeled on Palantir's deployment approach
items.detail: At AWS Summit Washington DC (June 30), Amazon announced a $1 billion Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) program putting thousands of engineers on-site with customers to co-develop AI solutions — a model explicitly described by industry analysts as inspired by Palantir's approach. The program aims to compress AI application development from months to days. FDE adds a new competitive dynamic in enterprise AI deployment services running on Azure.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations as of June 30. GPT-5.5 remains fully available. Confidential S-1 filed June 8; company leaning toward 2027 IPO. 42-state AG probe ongoing. ChatGPT global market share at 46.4% — first time below 50%. Jalapeño custom inference chip with Broadcom targeting H2 2026 Stargate deployment.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, Luna still restricted to ~20 government-vetted orgs; GPT-5.5 fully available
items.detail: As of June 30, GPT-5.6 Sol ($5/$30 per million input/output tokens), Terra ($2.50/$15), and Luna ($1/$6) remain in limited preview restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted partner organizations under U.S. government-imposed access requirements. GPT-5.5 is fully available to all users. GPT-4.5 was retired June 26 and replaced by GPT-5.5 in existing conversations. OpenAI hopes to make GPT-5.6 widely available 'in the coming weeks.'

items.headline: S-1 filed June 8; IPO leaning 2027; 42-state AG probe; ChatGPT market share 46.4% — first below 50%
items.detail: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC on June 8, 2026, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. The company is leaning toward a 2027 listing — CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility. A coalition of 42 state AGs (led by NY AG Letitia James) opened a formal probe June 12 covering user harm and advertising practices. Sensor Tower found ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share at 46.4% — first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

items.headline: Jalapeño custom ASIC with Broadcom targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment; Ona acquisition closed June 21
items.detail: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño on June 24 — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator, a reticle-sized ASIC designed from the ground up for LLM inference, built in nine months on TSMC advanced process with substantially better performance per watt than current alternatives. Initial deployment targets H2 2026 in Stargate data centers. OpenAI acquired Ona on June 21 for AI research and agentic workflow capabilities. Codex Remote is now generally available on all ChatGPT plans.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Anthropic held its 'AI for Science' virtual event today (June 30), launching Claude Science — an AI research workbench with 60+ scientific databases — and announcing an internal drug discovery program targeting neglected diseases. Fable 5 remains suspended on Day 18; the end-of-June restoration did not materialize. The $65B Series H at a $965B post-money valuation (led by Altimeter, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, Sequoia) is confirmed. ARR: $47B. October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target.

items.headline: Claude Science launched today at AI for Science event — research workbench with 60+ databases; drug discovery program announced
items.detail: At the 'The Briefing: AI for Science' virtual event today (June 30, 10 AM PST), Anthropic launched Claude Science — an AI research workbench combining databases, coding tools, compute, and research workflows in one workspace. Claude Science is pre-configured with more than 60 scientific databases and can render 3D protein structures, genome browser tracks, and chemistry drawings. Available in beta for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers on macOS and Linux. Anthropic also announced an internal drug discovery program targeting neglected diseases, and a grant program offering up to $30,000 in credits for 50 selected AI for Science research projects. Nobel laureate John Jumper appeared at the event in his first public appearance since joining from DeepMind.

items.headline: Fable 5 suspended Day 18 — end-of-June restoration did not materialize; Mythos 5 remains at ~100 orgs
items.detail: Fable 5 remains suspended for all general users — consumers, API developers, Claude Code, and international subscribers — on Day 18 as of June 30. The end-of-June restoration window expected from the June 27 Axios report did not materialize. Mythos 5 remains partially restored to approximately 100 trusted companies and federal agencies including critical infrastructure operators under the June 26 Commerce Secretary Lutnick letter. Prediction markets give roughly 57% odds of Fable 5 restoration by July, per Kalshi.

items.headline: Series H: $65B at $965B post-money valuation; $47B ARR; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target
items.detail: Anthropic raised $65 billion in Series H funding at a $965 billion post-money valuation, led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital, with co-investors including Capital Group, Coatue, D1 Capital Partners, GIC, ICONIQ, and XN. The round includes $15 billion of previously committed investments from hyperscalers including $5 billion from Amazon, plus strategic infrastructure partners Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix. ARR crossed $47 billion. The confidential S-1 filed June 1 targets an October 2026 Nasdaq listing with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley leading an offering expected to raise more than $60 billion.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta One paid AI subscription ($7.99/month Plus, $19.99/month Premium) is live. Meta Glasses launched June 23 at $299–$399 with strong early demand and 69.2% smart glasses market share. Meta is building Arena, a Llama-powered prediction market app. More than 1,600 employees signed a petition against AI monitoring of keystrokes. Meta reportedly hired hundreds of contractors to probe competing AI models on safety scenarios.

items.headline: Meta One paid AI subscription live: $7.99 Plus / $19.99 Premium across Family of Apps
items.detail: Meta's Meta One paid AI subscription is live, offering enhanced capabilities across WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, Messenger, and Threads. Meta One Plus costs $7.99 per month; Meta One Premium costs $19.99 per month. The offering targets power users and positions Meta AI as a competitor to ChatGPT Plus and Claude Pro in the consumer AI subscription market. This is part of Meta's revenue diversification strategy beyond advertising.

items.headline: Meta Glasses launched June 23 ($299–$399); AI Mode live on Facebook; Arena prediction market app in development
items.detail: Meta's in-house Meta Glasses (launched June 23 with EssilorLuxottica at $299–$399) show strong early sales with smart glasses shipments up 167% YoY in Q1 2026 and Meta at 69.2% market share. AI Mode launched on Facebook, pulling synthesized answers from public posts across Groups and Reels. Meta CEO Zuckerberg is building Arena (codenames: Antwerp/FBForecast), a standalone Llama-powered prediction market app competing with Kalshi and Polymarket. Meta's 2026 capex guidance of $115–135 billion remains unchanged.

items.headline: Meta reportedly hired contractors to probe competitor AI models on safety; 1,600+ employees sign keystroke-monitoring petition
items.detail: Meta reportedly hired hundreds of contractors to simulate children and probe how competing AI models — including those from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google — respond to prompts involving suicide, sex, and high-risk scenarios, revealing intensifying AI safety competition among Big Tech. Separately, more than 1,600 Meta employees signed a petition against a program monitoring clicks and keystrokes for AI training data, reflecting internal tension over surveillance practices.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS Summit Washington DC opened today (June 30–July 1) with major federal and defense AI announcements: $1B IC Accelerated Modernization Framework for the U.S. Intelligence Community, AWS Secret Cloud for Industry (ASCI) for defense contractors, and a $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program. CIA Director Ratcliffe confirmed CIA will use ICAMF. Amazon is pursuing a $3 trillion valuation with AWS AI as the key driver.

items.headline: AWS Summit DC: $1B ICAMF for intelligence community; CIA Director Ratcliffe confirms CIA participation
items.detail: At AWS Summit Washington DC today, AWS launched the IC Accelerated Modernization Framework (ICAMF), a $1 billion program designed to eliminate migration costs for U.S. Intelligence Community agencies by providing credits for workloads migrated to AWS. CIA Director John Ratcliffe — in his first public remarks in office — confirmed CIA will take advantage of ICAMF and said LLMs are already helping analysts surface hidden intelligence and translate content that would have taken hundreds of hours. The program is available through October 2030 for all IC agencies on the existing AWS contract.

items.headline: AWS Secret Cloud for Industry (ASCI) launches; $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program announced
items.detail: AWS launched AWS Secret Cloud for Industry (ASCI), giving defense contractors access to classified cloud infrastructure for contractor-owned workloads for the first time. AWS also committed $1 billion to a new Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) organization, placing thousands of engineers on-site with customers to co-develop AI solutions — a model explicitly modeled on Palantir's successful deployment strategy. The FDE program aims to compress AI application development timelines from months to days.

items.headline: Amazon $13B India investment (Mumbai/Hyderabad); Amazon Quick launched; Amazon eyes $3T valuation
items.detail: Amazon announced a $13 billion investment to expand AWS AI and cloud infrastructure in Mumbai and Hyderabad, delivering custom AI chips, managed AI services, and developer tools. Amazon Quick — an AI assistant for work with a desktop app — launched alongside expanded Amazon Connect agentic AI solutions for supply chain, hiring, customer experience, and healthcare. AWS WAF added AI traffic monetization capability. Amazon is targeting a $3 trillion valuation with AWS AI as the primary driver.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts tomorrow (July 1), with both Colossus facilities fully committed to third parties. Grok 4.5 (1.5T parameters, V9 model, trained on Cursor data) is in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX. SPCX trades at approximately $153 — still 32% below its June 16 ATH of $225.64. The $60B Cursor acquisition remains on track for Q3 2026 close. Grok adult content CSAM disclosure remains an unresolved regulatory risk.

items.headline: Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts tomorrow (July 1); both Colossus facilities fully committed
items.detail: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI) starts tomorrow (July 1) at $150 million per month for NVIDIA GB300 GPU access via Colossus 2 in Memphis through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Combined with Colossus 1 rented to Anthropic at approximately $1.25 billion per month, both Colossus facilities are now committed to third parties. Grok 4.5 in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX raises questions about xAI's own future dedicated compute access.

items.headline: Grok 4.5 in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX — 1.5T parameters, V9 model, trained on Cursor coding data
items.detail: xAI's Grok 4.5 is running in private beta inside Tesla and SpaceX as of late June 2026. The model is built on xAI's V9 foundation (1.5 trillion parameters, training completed May 26) and incorporates supplemental training on Cursor AI coding data — integrating assets from the $60 billion pending acquisition ahead of its formal Q3 2026 close. Early evaluations place performance close to or above Opus. Monthly launch cadence planned.

items.headline: Grok adult content tops 10B images/month; xAI engineers admit CSAM has no current technical fix; Cursor Q3 close on track
items.detail: Grok's adult content generation is producing more than 10 billion images per month, with SpaceX's IPO prospectus setting aside $530 million to cover potential related litigation. xAI engineers publicly acknowledged that CSAM generation is an unsolved technical problem with no current fix, drawing criticism from child safety advocates and lawmakers. The $60B Cursor acquisition — announced June 16 — remains on track for Q3 2026 close pending regulatory approvals. SpaceX said Cursor has 2M+ developers and approximately $2.6B in annual B2B revenue.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: Broadcom's $0.65/share quarterly dividend was paid today (June 30) — 14 consecutive years of increases. OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño on June 24 — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator, a reticle-sized ASIC built in 9 months for LLM inference targeting H2 2026 Stargate deployment. Q2 AI semiconductor revenue: $10.8B (+143% YoY). FY2026 full-year guidance: $56B. FY2027 target: $100B+. The $35B Apollo/Blackstone AI XPV Platform is operational.

items.headline: Broadcom $0.65/share quarterly dividend paid today — 14 consecutive years of dividend increases
items.detail: Broadcom's $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend was paid today (June 30, 2026) to stockholders of record at the close of business on June 22, 2026. This marks 14 consecutive years of quarterly dividend increases. Q2 FY2026 total revenue was a record $22.2 billion (+48% YoY), with AI semiconductor revenue of $10.8 billion (+143% YoY). Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance is approximately $16 billion. Full-year FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue guidance: $56 billion. FY2027 target: more than $100 billion.

items.headline: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil Jalapeño — first custom OpenAI ASIC built in 9 months; H2 2026 Stargate target
items.detail: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño on June 24 — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator chip — co-developed from initial design to manufacturing tape-out in nine months, described as the fastest ASIC development cycle in high-performance advanced semiconductors. Jalapeño is a reticle-sized ASIC designed from the ground up for LLM inference, offering substantially better performance per watt than current state-of-the-art. Initial deployment targets H2 2026 in Stargate data centers. Broadcom CEO Hock Tan confirmed OpenAI as Broadcom's sixth custom-silicon customer.

items.headline: Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform operational; enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU compute through 2028
items.detail: The Apollo and Blackstone-led $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform is operational (since June 9), enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The platform facilitates Anthropic's 1+ gigawatt compute expansion at Fluidstack-based sites beginning mid-2026. Broadcom's Tomahawk 6 (Davisson) — the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics — is the networking layer for the next AI compute generation.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: Amazon's $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program announced today at AWS Summit DC was explicitly described as inspired by Palantir's AI deployment model — a backhanded validation of Palantir's approach. PLTR trades around $112–114, recovering from the 52-week low of $107.27 on June 25. Wedbush set a $230 price target (roughly 100% upside). Q1 2026 revenue: $1.632B (+84.7% YoY) — highest growth rate in company history. 2026 federal wins: Army $10B, DHS $1B, Treasury (June 23).

items.headline: Amazon's $1B FDE program at AWS Summit DC modeled on Palantir's deployment approach
items.detail: At AWS Summit Washington DC today, Amazon announced a $1 billion Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) program — putting thousands of engineers on-site with customers to co-develop AI solutions — explicitly described by analysts as modeled on Palantir's successful AI deployment strategy. The initiative compresses AI application development from months to days. This represents a competitive encroachment into Palantir's differentiated deployment model, but also validates the approach Palantir has executed for years.

items.headline: PLTR recovering from 52-week low; Wedbush $230 price target; Q1 revenue $1.632B +84.7% YoY
items.detail: Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives reiterated a bullish Palantir outlook with a $230 12-month price target — implying nearly 100% upside from the recent level around $112–114. PLTR hit a 52-week low of $107.27 on June 25 before partially recovering. Q1 2026 results posted $1.632 billion revenue (+84.7% YoY) — the highest growth rate in company history. U.S. commercial revenue surged 133% to $595 million. Management raised full-year guidance to approximately 71% growth — a 10-point jump from prior guidance.

items.headline: Treasury contract (June 23) joins Army $10B and DHS $1B; Mythos 5 partial restoration improves defense pipeline
items.detail: Palantir won a U.S. Treasury Department contract on June 23 for a common API layer supporting developer platforms, workflow automation, and data analytics. 2026 federal contract wins: $10B 10-year Army enterprise AI services, $1B 5-year DHS AI platform, $300M USDA deal (April), and the Treasury contract. Mythos 5's partial restoration to critical infrastructure and government operators on June 26 improves the outlook for the Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Pro's confirmed July delay adds new delivery risk to Apple's Gemini-powered Siri AI ahead of the September 2026 GA launch. iOS 27 developer beta continues since WWDC June 8; public beta expected mid-July. OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice. Apple is evaluating Mistral as an additional model option alongside Gemini.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro July delay adds delivery risk to Apple's Gemini-powered Siri AI (September 2026 GA)
items.detail: Google's confirmed delay of Gemini 3.5 Pro to July 2026 directly constrains the capability ceiling of Apple's Gemini-powered Siri AI ahead of the September 2026 general availability launch. Google's DeepMind talent exodus — five senior researchers departed in one week — compounds the delivery risk. Apple's $1 billion/year Gemini deal (announced at WWDC June 8) makes it dependent on Google delivering a refined model before Apple's September iPhone launch window.

items.headline: iOS 27 Gemini-powered Siri AI in developer beta since WWDC June 8; public beta mid-July; GA September 2026
items.detail: At WWDC June 8, Apple unveiled iOS 27 with a fully rebuilt Siri AI powered by Google's Gemini model (~1.2 trillion parameters), plus next-generation Apple Intelligence with personal context AI and Visual Intelligence. Siri AI features a dedicated standalone app, Dynamic Island integration, full on-screen awareness, and multi-step task execution via a three-tier system: on-device, Private Cloud Compute, and Gemini for complex tasks. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) is disabled pending EU DMA regulatory clarity. GA: September 2026.

items.headline: OpenAI weighs formal breach-of-contract notice over Siri placement; Apple evaluating Mistral as additional model
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over the perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement in the new Siri AI, alleging Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments. No lawsuit has been filed as of June 30. Sherwood News reports Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option alongside Gemini, suggesting ongoing hedging across providers. Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions remain confirmed dead.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: MI450 sampling is confirmed to lead customers OpenAI and Meta. Oracle announced a 50,000-unit MI450 deployment for the first publicly available AI supercluster in Q3 2026. MI450 specs: 432GB HBM4 memory, 20 TB/s bandwidth, 40 PFLOPs FP4 per GPU. MI450 demand has already exceeded AMD's own 2027 internal projections. AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22–23 at Moscone Center; Q2 2026 results report August 4.

items.headline: Oracle deploys 50,000 MI450 GPUs for public AI supercluster Q3 2026; MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta
items.detail: Oracle announced a 50,000-unit AMD Instinct MI450 GPU deployment for the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster, with initial deployment in Q3 2026 and planned 2027 expansion. AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to lead customers OpenAI (6GW partnership, beginning with 1GW MI450 in H2 2026) and Meta ($60B/6GW). MI450 features 432GB HBM4 memory, 20 TB/s bandwidth, 40 PFLOPs FP4, and 20 PFLOPs FP8 per GPU. MI450 demand has exceeded AMD's own internal 2027 projections due to OAM-based inference deployments.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 on July 22–23 at Moscone Center; MI500 engagement underway; Q2 results August 4
items.detail: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22–23 at Moscone Center in San Francisco — is expected to formally launch MI450, preview EPYC Venice (Zen 6) CPUs, confirm Helios rack-scale systems, and offer a first look at the MI500 series targeting 2027. AMD CEO confirmed active customer engagement on MI500, noting that the largest AI deployments are increasingly for inference at scale. Analysts project the MI400 series will generate approximately $7.2 billion in revenue in its first year, roughly 25% of AMD's total data center sales. Q2 2026 financial results: August 4.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Perplexity launched Personal Computer — an always-on AI running on a dedicated Mac mini — as the next step beyond Comet browser. Comet browser now available for Enterprise with MDM deployment and Claude Opus 4.6 as the default model. CoreWeave powers Perplexity's inference workloads on dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters. ARR: $500M (+335% YoY); 100M+ MAU; $20B valuation; 2028 IPO target firm.

items.headline: Perplexity Personal Computer launched — always-on AI on dedicated Mac mini; Comet now available for Enterprise
items.detail: Perplexity announced Personal Computer — an always-on AI that runs on a dedicated Mac mini, merging local files, apps, and sessions with Perplexity Computer to monitor triggers, execute proactive tasks, and carry work forward around the clock. Voice Mode brings natural conversation to Personal Computer. Comet browser is now available for Enterprise organizations, with MDM deployment across macOS and Windows, and hundreds of configurable browser policies. Comet now defaults to Claude Opus 4.6 for complex reasoning tasks.

items.headline: 2028 IPO target firm; $500M ARR; 100M MAU; $20B valuation; CoreWeave GB200 NVL72 inference partnership
items.detail: CEO Aravind Srinivas reaffirmed a 2028 IPO target, explicitly not accelerating despite Anthropic and OpenAI moving toward public markets. Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are confirmed dead. ARR reached $500 million (+335% YoY) with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20B valuation. CoreWeave's multi-year strategic partnership provides dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72-powered clusters for Perplexity's inference workloads, with NVIDIA Rubin-based systems beginning H2 2026. Perplexity Computer runs as native side panels in Microsoft 365 apps. A $750M Azure deal provides cloud infrastructure.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CRWV fell 5.1% today to close at approximately $95.95 after Evercore ISI revised its model with materially worse EPS estimates (CY26: -$5.12 vs -$3.95; CY27: -$4.30 vs -$1.14), citing conservative deferred tax asset recognition and higher projected interest expense. CoreWeave ARIA — an AI research agent built into Weights & Biases — entered public preview today. Revenue backlog surged 50% sequentially to $99.4B; FY2026 revenue guidance reaffirmed at $12–13B. Russell 3000 inclusion effective June 26 continues to drive passive-fund buying.

items.headline: CRWV falls 5.1% to ~$95.95 — Evercore ISI revises EPS estimates materially worse for CY26 and CY27
items.detail: CoreWeave shares fell 5.1% in morning trading on June 30 after Evercore ISI adjusted its financial model, revising its CY26 EPS forecast to -$5.12 from -$3.95 and its CY27 estimate to -$4.30 from -$1.14, citing the company's conservative approach to deferred tax asset recognition and higher projected interest expenses. CRWV closed at approximately $95.95 (range $89.56–$99.26) on June 30. The 37-analyst average Buy rating with a $143.41 price target remains intact. Russell 3000 inclusion effective June 26 continues driving institutional buying (Vanguard +275.6%, PNC +248.9%).

items.headline: CoreWeave ARIA enters public preview — AI research agent built into Weights & Biases
items.detail: CoreWeave launched CoreWeave ARIA (AI Research & Iteration Agent) into public preview today, built directly into Weights & Biases using W&B Weave's agent development platform. ARIA can work through thousands of experiment runs and tens of thousands of metrics in minutes, surfacing insights researchers might miss and recommending ways to improve models and agents. W&B Weave's agent development capabilities also enter general availability today. Rosenblatt said to buy CoreWeave stock as it 'looks increasingly like the de facto operating system for AI.'

items.headline: Backlog $99.4B (+50% QoQ); FY2026 guidance $12–13B; $18–19B exit run rate; 9 of top 10 AI labs on platform
items.detail: CoreWeave reaffirmed FY2026 revenue guidance of $12–13 billion with an $18–19 billion exit run rate. Revenue backlog surged 50% sequentially to $99.4 billion, driven by Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout for Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI. Nine of the top ten AI model providers are CoreWeave customers. The $3.1B HPC loan closed recently. 2026 capex floor: $31 billion. The Perplexity multi-year GB200 NVL72 partnership adds dedicated AI search inference capacity.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs) missed its end-of-June opening target — no official opening announcement was made as of June 30. OCR 4 (June 23) covers 170 languages with a 72% average win rate. Funding discussions continue at approximately €20B ($23.2B) valuation targeting a $3.5B raise. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral for future Siri/iOS integration. Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition.

items.headline: Paris data center misses end-of-June target — 44MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs; no opening announcement made
items.detail: Mistral AI's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris — 44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems providing approximately 276 exaflops of FP4 compute, operated by Eclairion — missed its end-of-June opening target with no official announcement made as of June 30. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10 MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. The Bruyères-le-Châtel facility is the flagship of Mistral's planned 200 MW European compute roadmap. Combined with a $1.4B Sweden deal, Mistral targets 200 MW across Europe by end of 2027.

items.headline: OCR 4 launched June 23 — 170 languages, 72% win rate, $4/1K pages; Apple evaluating Mistral for Siri
items.detail: Mistral AI released OCR 4 on June 23, a document intelligence model covering 170 languages with paragraph-level bounding boxes, deployable as a single container for regulated enterprises. Independent annotators prefer OCR 4 over every leading OCR and document-AI system tested, with win rates averaging 72%, and the top overall score on OlmOCRBench (85.20). Available via API at $4 per 1,000 pages. Sherwood News reports Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option alongside Gemini for future Siri/iOS integration. Mistral Compute launched at VivaTech 2026 (June 20) with NVIDIA — a sovereign AI GPU cloud built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems.

items.headline: Funding discussions at ~€20B ($23.2B) valuation; $3.5B raise target; CEO targets €1B 2026 revenue
items.detail: Mistral AI is in discussions with investors about raising approximately €3 billion ($3.5 billion) at a potential valuation near €20 billion ($23.2 billion), per Bloomberg. CEO Arthur Mensch targets €1 billion in 2026 revenue, up from €200 million in 2025. An $830M debt facility from Bpifrance and consortium banks funds the current data center buildout. Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition alongside other open frontier model builders.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: A Tesla executive stated the Cybercab will be sold to customers for under $30,000 within the next six months, suggesting volume deliveries before end of 2026. Optimus Gen 3 is in mass production at Fremont; V3 full-body production targets this summer with first external commercial customers targeted for late 2026. Giga Texas North targets 10 million robots per year. Grok 4.5 private beta is running inside Tesla using Cursor training data.

items.headline: Tesla executive: Cybercab will be under $30K within 6 months; EPA confirms 418-mile unadjusted range
items.detail: A Tesla executive stated the Cybercab will be sold to customers for under $30,000 within the next six months, placing volume deliveries before the end of 2026 — suggesting production at Gigafactory Texas is further along than public commentary has implied. EPA filing (June 19) confirmed production Cybercab specs: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range (293-mile adjusted), 3,113 lb curb weight. The active Cybercab fleet spans approximately 59–100+ units across Austin, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. Elon Musk told investors to be 'really patient' on ramp timelines.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 in mass production at Fremont; V3 targets summer 2026; external B2B customers late 2026; Terafab AI5 chip initiative
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot is in mass production at Fremont, with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026. First external commercial customers — enterprise/B2B deployments — are targeted for late 2026. Tesla's Terafab initiative targets in-house semiconductor fabrication for the AI5 chip powering Optimus Gen 3's onboard AI, FSD, and Cybercab systems. The Gigafactory Texas North Campus expansion (5.2 million square feet) targets 10 million robots per year long term. FSD v14.3 is deployed with 20% reduced inference latency. Grok 4.5 is running in private beta inside Tesla.

ipoTracker.status: Claude Science launched today (AI for Science event); Fable 5 suspended Day 18 — end-of-June restoration missed; Series H $65B at $965B; $47B ARR; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, targeting an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO. The $65 billion Series H closed at a $965 billion post-money valuation, led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital — including $15 billion from hyperscalers (including $5 billion from Amazon) and strategic chip partners Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix. ARR: $47 billion. Claude Science launched today at the AI for Science virtual event; John Jumper appeared. Fable 5 remains suspended on Day 18 with Kalshi markets giving 57% odds of restoration by July. Mythos 5 remains at approximately 100 trusted organizations. S-1 underwriters: Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley; expected raise: $60B+.
ipoTracker.note: Claude Science launch today is the most significant positive development. Fable 5 Day 18 suspension is the dominant pre-IPO operational risk. Series H now confirmed at $65B/$965B — larger than previously reported $30B+ figure.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic raises $65B in Series H funding at $965B post-money valuation

GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations as of June 30. GPT-4.5 was retired June 26. A coalition of 42 state AGs opened a formal probe June 12. ChatGPT's global market share fell to 46.4% — first time below 50%, with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

partnershipTracker.status: Active strategic relationship; MI300X on Azure; Copilot Cowork billing tomorrow adds Azure AI demand; AMD Advancing AI July 22–23
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD MI300X GPU instances are deployed on Azure for AI inference workloads alongside NVIDIA hardware. Microsoft IQ (Work IQ + Web IQ) is GA across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio. MAI-Code-1 and MAI-Code-1-Flash are Microsoft in-house models for code generation on Azure. Azure Cobalt 200 VMs deliver 50% performance improvement. Copilot Cowork billing activates tomorrow (July 1), driving additional Azure AI workloads. AMD Advancing AI 2026 (July 22–23) may include additional Azure-related deployment disclosures.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Ongoing relationship; no new announced terms
partnershipTracker.note: Copilot Cowork billing tomorrow (July 1) activates additional Azure AI compute demand. AMD Advancing AI July 22–23 is the next potential milestone.

latestArticle.title: AMD at Microsoft Build 2026

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed $60B, ~6GW multi-year agreement; MI450 samples shipping to Meta; Meta One adds AI inference demand; AMD Advancing AI July 22–23
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD and Meta's 6-gigawatt, roughly $60 billion strategic partnership covers multiple generations of AMD Instinct GPUs co-developed via the Helios architecture through the Open Compute Project, with shipments beginning H2 2026. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to Meta. The agreement includes a 160-million-share AMD warrant giving Meta a potential 10% stake. Meta One paid subscription ($7.99–$19.99/month) adds consumer AI inference workloads that will scale into AMD-powered infrastructure.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies; samples shipping per AMD CEO
partnershipTracker.note: Meta One subscription adds consumer AI inference demand. AMD Advancing AI July 22–23 may include Meta deployment specifics.

latestArticle.title: AMD and Meta Announce Expanded Strategic Partnership to Deploy 6 Gigawatts of AMD GPUs

partnershipTracker.type: Infrastructure equity investment and multi-generational GPU supply
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed deep strategic partnership; CRWV fell 5.1% today on Evercore ISI revision; Russell 3000 effective June 26; NVIDIA 8-K filed today; ARIA launch diversifies CoreWeave platform
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA invested roughly $2 billion in CoreWeave Class A common stock (~11% stake) and committed to accelerating more than 5GW of AI factories by 2030. CoreWeave completed the industry-first Vera Rubin NVL72 bring-up on June 1. Backlog $99.4 billion (+50% QoQ). CRWV fell 5.1% to approximately $95.95 today on Evercore ISI model revision — CY26 EPS revised to -$5.12 from -$3.95. NVIDIA Annual Meeting 8-K proxy results filed today. CoreWeave ARIA entered public preview today.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies
partnershipTracker.note: Evercore ISI revision today is the key near-term negative development. CRWV at ~$96 vs $143 analyst average target. NVIDIA 8-K filed today — no change to GPU supply relationship.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave ARIA Launches as an AI Research and Iteration Agent

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed partnership; Paris data center missed end-of-June opening target; Apple reportedly evaluating Mistral; OCR 4 live; Mistral Compute launched at VivaTech
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (June 20). Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition. Mistral's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 systems) missed its end-of-June opening target with no announcement as of June 30. Mistral OCR 4 launched June 23. Funding talks at ~€20B ($23.2B) valuation support further scale.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies at VivaTech
partnershipTracker.note: Paris data center missed end-of-June target today. Opening now expected in July. Apple's reported Mistral evaluation is a potential enterprise distribution catalyst.

latestArticle.title: France Advances Europe's AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies

partnershipTracker.type: AI compute contract — Colossus 2 data center access
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed $6.3B deal — $150M/month starts tomorrow (July 1); Colossus 2 dedicated to Reflection AI
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceX signed a $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI), providing access to NVIDIA GB300 GPUs via the Colossus 2 data center in Memphis. Reflection pays $150 million per month starting tomorrow (July 1) through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Combined with Colossus 1 rented to Anthropic, both Colossus facilities are committed to third parties.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by CNBC reporting
partnershipTracker.note: July 1 start date is tomorrow — payments begin. This completes the monetization of both Colossus facilities to third parties, raising questions about xAI's own future dedicated compute access for Grok.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX AI Colossus data center signs deal with Reflection AI

partnershipTracker.type: Structured finance platform for Broadcom AI XPU deployment
partnershipTracker.status: Operational — $35B capital solution; enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU compute; Broadcom dividend paid today; Jalapeño reinforces Broadcom's six-customer silicon position
partnershipTracker.terms: Apollo and Blackstone are leading a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform, enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The platform is operational since June 9. The OpenAI-Broadcom Jalapeño chip (June 24) confirms OpenAI as Broadcom's sixth custom-silicon customer alongside Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic. Broadcom's $0.65 quarterly dividend was paid today (June 30). FY2027 $100B+ AI revenue target reinforces platform scale.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Apollo press release and Broadcom announcement
partnershipTracker.note: Broadcom dividend paid today. FY2027 $100B+ AI revenue target confirms XPU platform is on a significant long-term growth trajectory. Six custom-silicon customers now confirmed.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Broadcom reveal Jalapeño, first AI chip in partnership
